vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The visual data indicates a critical failure in the biome. The expected specimens (P1-P4) are not visible or are in a state of total senescence/decomposition. The only identifiable physical anchor is the white rabbit figurine in the black pot.

inventory_reconciliation.P1_String_of_Nickels: Systemic Loss: No foliage detected in the yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2_Mexican_Mint: Systemic Loss: No foliage detected in the black pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P3_Pothos: Systemic Loss: Only the white rabbit anchor remains; no plant matter visible.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears dark and potentially desiccated or saturated with organic decay; no moss or secondary growth.
biome_observations.surface_debris: Minimal, consistent with a neglected or failed botanical environment.
biome_observations.lighting_impact: The cool spectrum LED reveals a lack of chlorophyll-bearing tissue across all containers.

temporal_deltas.EARLIEST_to_T-1: Total loss of any visible green matter. The environment transitioned from a potential growth state to a barren state.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Static. No recovery or new growth observed. The biome remains in a state of total systemic collapse.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is non-viable. All registered plants have suffered total mortality. The absence of any photosynthetic material confirms the death of the specimens.
vision_report.anomalies: The primary anomaly is the complete disappearance of all plant life, leaving only the rabbit anchor.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit confirms a catastrophic loss of the botanical collection. Across the three-image sequence, there is no evidence of surviving plant matter. The pots contain only soil and the white rabbit anchor. The environment is currently devoid of any living botanical specimens.
